triggers.name: Emontx → On Data
triggers.description: An automation that will be triggered for every serial line received from the EmonTx (both plain text and JSON). Within this trigger, the `data` variable (string type) contains the received line. This is useful for debugging, logging all serial output, or handling configuration responses from the EmonTx.

triggers.name: Emontx → On Json
triggers.description: An automation that will be triggered whenever new JSON data is received from the EmonTx. Within this trigger, the `raw_json` variable (string type) contains the received JSON data as a string. A parsed JSON object is also available as the `json` variable (JsonObject type), which can be used to access and manipulate specific fields in the JSON data.

triggers.name: Key Collector → On Progress
triggers.description: An automation to perform when keys are pressed. The current key sequence is placed in a `vector<uint8_t>` variable `x` and `start` holds the start key that activated this key sequence or else `0`. Useful if you want to have a display showing the current value or number of key presses, or a speaker beeping when keys are being pressed.

triggers.name: Key Collector → On Result
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the key sequence has been finished (eg. `max_length` has been reached or one of the `end_keys` was pressed). The finalized key sequence is placed in a `vector<uint8_t>` variable `x`, `start` holds the start key that activated this key sequence or else `0`, and `end` holds the end key that terminated this key sequence or else `0`.

triggers.name: Key Collector → On Timeout
triggers.description: An automation to perform if the timeout happens. The current key sequence is placed in a `vector<uint8_t>` variable `x` and `start` holds the start key that activated this key sequence or else `0`.

triggers.description: An automation to perform when the load is switched. If a lambda is used the boolean `state` parameter holds the new status.

triggers.name: Output.Sigma Delta Output → Turn Off Action
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the load is turned off. `turn_on_action` and `turn_off_action` must be configured together.

triggers.name: Output.Sigma Delta Output → Turn On Action
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the load is turned on. Can be used to control for example a switch or output component.

triggers.name: Sim800L → On Call Connected
triggers.description: An action to be performed when a call is connected, either because an outgoing call accepted is accepted or an incoming call answered.

triggers.name: Sim800L → On Call Disconnected
triggers.description: An action to be performed when a call is disconnected.

triggers.name: Sim800L → On Incoming Call
triggers.description: An action to be performed when a call is received. See [`on_incoming_call` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/sim800l#sim800l-on_incoming_call).

triggers.name: Sim800L → On Sms Received
triggers.description: An action to be performed when an SMS is received. See [`on_sms_received` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/sim800l#sim800l-on_sms_received).

triggers.name: Output.Slow Pwm → State Change Action
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the load is switched. If a lambda is used the boolean `state` parameter holds the new status.

triggers.description: An automation to perform when the load is turned off. `turn_on_action` and `turn_off_action` must be configured together.

triggers.name: Output.Slow Pwm → Turn On Action
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the load is turned on. Can be used to control for example a switch or output component.

triggers.name: Media Player.Speaker → On Unmute
triggers.description: An automation to perform when unmuted.

triggers.name: Sun → On Sunset

triggers.name: Switch → On Turn Off

triggers.name: Switch → On Turn On

triggers.name: Sx126X → On Packet
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a packet has been decoded. Variable x of type `std::vector<uint8_t>` and rssi of type `float` are passed to the automation for use in lambdas. In LoRa mode the variable snr is also available.

triggers.name: Sx127X → On Packet
triggers.description: An automation to perform in packet mode when a packet has been decoded. A variable x of type `std::vector<uint8_t>` is passed to the automation for use in lambdas. In LoRa mode the variables snr and rssi are also available.

triggers.name: Cover.Template → Close Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be closed.

triggers.name: Cover.Template → Open Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the cover to be opened.

triggers.name: Cover.Template → Position Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the cover be set to a specific position. The desired position is available in the lambda in the `pos` variable. Used only when `has_position` is set to `true`.

triggers.name: Cover.Template → Stop Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be stopped.

triggers.name: Cover.Template → Tilt Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the cover be set to a specific tilt position. The desired tilt is available in the lambda in the `tilt` variable.

triggers.name: Cover.Template → Toggle Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests to toggle the cover.

triggers.name: Datetime.Template → Set Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests to set the dateime value. The new value is available to lambdas in the `x` variable.

triggers.name: Lock.Template → Lock Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the lock to be locked.

triggers.name: Lock.Template → Unlock Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the lock to be unlocked.

triggers.name: Number.Template → Set Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests to set the number value. The new value is available to lambdas in the `x` variable.

triggers.name: Select.Template → Set Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests to set the Select option. The new option is available to lambdas in the `x` variable.

triggers.name: Switch.Template → Turn Off Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the switch to be turned off.

triggers.name: Switch.Template → Turn On Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the switch to be turned on.

triggers.name: Text.Template → Set Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests to set the text value. The new value is available to lambdas in the `x` variable.

triggers.name: Valve.Template → Close Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the valve to be closed.

triggers.name: Valve.Template → Open Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the valve to be opened.

triggers.name: Valve.Template → Position Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the valve be set to a specific position. The desired position is available in the lambda in the `pos` variable. Requires `has_position` (above) to be set to `true`.

triggers.name: Valve.Template → Stop Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the valve to be stopped.

triggers.name: Water Heater.Template → Set Action
triggers.description: The action to perform when the water heater receives a command (mode change, target temperature change, away, on/off, etc.). This is where you implement the actual control logic for your water heater.

triggers.name: Text → On Value
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a new value is published. See [`on_value`](https://esphome.io/components/text#text-on_value).

triggers.name: Text Sensor → On Raw Value
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a new value is received that hasn't passed through any filters. See [`on_raw_value`](https://esphome.io/components/text_sensor#text_sensor-on_raw_value).

triggers.name: Text Sensor → On Value
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a new value is published. See [`on_value`](https://esphome.io/components/text_sensor#text_sensor-on_value).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Auto Mode
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into "auto" mode (it may both cool and heat as required). This mode is different from `heat_cool_mode` (above) in that it takes control of the set points away from the user; it's generally intended for exclusive use by automations.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Cool Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device should enter cooling mode to decrease the current temperature.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Cool Mode
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into cool mode (it may cool as required, but not heat).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Dry Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device should perform its drying (dehumidification) action. The thermostat controller does not trigger this action; it is invoked by `dry_mode` (see below).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Dry Mode
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into dry mode (for dehumidification).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Auto Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should be set to "auto" mode (the fan is controlled by the climate control system as required).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Diffuse Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should direct its airflow over a broad area.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Focus Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should direct its airflow at a specific area.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode High Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should run at its maximum speed.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Low Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should run at its minimum speed.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Medium Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should run at an intermediate speed.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Middle Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should direct its airflow at an intermediate area.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Off Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should never run.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode On Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should run continuously.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Mode Quiet Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should run at quiet speed.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Only Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device should activate its fan only (but does not heat or cool). When `fan_only_cooling` is set to `false`, the thermostat controller immediately triggers this action when set to `fan_only_mode`  ; however, when `fan_only_cooling` is set to `true`, this action is called based on the upper target temperature (similar to `cool_action` above).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Fan Only Mode
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into fan-only mode (it may not heat or cool, but will activate its fan either immediately or, when `fan_only_cooling` is `true`, as needed based on the upper target temperature value).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Heat Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device should enter heating mode to increase the current temperature.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Heat Mode
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into heat mode (it may heat as required, but not cool).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Humidity Control Dehumidify Action
triggers.description: The action to call when dehumidification is required.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Humidity Control Humidify Action
triggers.description: The action to call when humidification is required.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Humidity Control Off Action
triggers.description: The action to call when (de)humidification should stop. This action is **required** when either of the above actions are configured.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Idle Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device should enter its idle state (not cooling, not heating).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Off Mode
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into "off" mode (it is completely disabled).

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Preset Change
triggers.description: (*Optional*, [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions)): The action to call when the preset is changed. This will be called either when a user changes the mode through the Home Assistant UI or through a call to `climate.control`

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Supplemental Cooling Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device should activate supplemental cooling to (more aggressively) decrease the current temperature. *This action is called repeatedly at an interval defined by* `max_cooling_run_time` *(see below).*

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Supplemental Heating Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the climate device should activate supplemental heating to (more aggressively) increase the current temperature. *This action is called repeatedly at an interval defined by* `max_heating_run_time` *(see below).*

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Swing Both Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should oscillate in horizontal and vertical directions.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Swing Horizontal Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should oscillate in a horizontal direction.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Swing Off Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should remain in a stationary position.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Swing Vertical Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the fan should oscillate in a vertical direction.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Target Humidity Change Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the thermostat's target humidity is changed.

triggers.name: Climate.Thermostat → Target Temperature Change Action
triggers.description: The action to call when the thermostat's target temperature(s) is/are changed.

triggers.name: Time → On Time
triggers.description: Automation to run at specific intervals using a cron-like syntax. See [`on_time` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/time#time-on_time).

triggers.name: Time → On Time Sync
triggers.description: Automation to run when the time source could be (re-)synchronized.. See [`on_time_sync` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/time#time-on_time_sync).

triggers.name: Cover.Time Based → Close Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be closed.

triggers.name: Cover.Time Based → Open Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be opened.

triggers.name: Cover.Time Based → Stop Action
triggers.description: The action that should be performed to stop the cover when the remote requests the cover to be stopped or when the cover has been opening/closing for the given durations.

triggers.name: Touchscreen → On Release
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the touchscreen is no longer touched. See [`on_release` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/touchscreen#touchscreen-on_release).

triggers.name: Touchscreen → On Touch
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the touchscreen is touched. See [`on_touch` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/touchscreen#touchscreen-on_touch).

triggers.name: Touchscreen → On Update
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the touchscreen is touched. See [`on_update` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/touchscreen#touchscreen-on_update).

triggers.name: Tuya → On Datapoint Update
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a Tuya datapoint update is received. See [`on_datapoint_update`](https://esphome.io/components/tuya#tuya-on_datapoint_update).

triggers.name: Update → On Update Available
triggers.description: An automation to perform when an update is available.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Client Connected
triggers.description: An automation to perform when Home Assistant has connected and is waiting for Voice Assistant commands.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Client Disconnected
triggers.description: An automation to perform when Home Assistant disconnects from the Voice Assistant.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On End
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant is finished all tasks.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Error
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant has encountered an error. The error code and message are available to automations as the variables `code` and `message`.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Idle
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant is idle (no other actions/states are in progress).

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Intent End
triggers.description: An automation to perform when intent processing ends.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Intent Progress
triggers.description: An automation to perform when intent progress happens. The variable `x` is a non-empty string containing the streaming TTS response URL only if it is sent to the media player.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Intent Start
triggers.description: An automation to perform when intent processing starts.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Listening
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant microphone starts listening.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Start
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the assist pipeline is started.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Stt End
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant has finished speech-to-text. The resulting text is available to automations as the variable `x`.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Stt Vad End
triggers.description: An automation to perform when voice activity detection ends speech-to-text processing.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Stt Vad Start
triggers.description: An automation to perform when voice activity detection starts speech-to-text processing.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Timer Cancelled
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a voice assistant timer has been cancelled. The timer is available as `timer` of type <APIStruct text="voice_assistant::Timer" path="voice_assistant::Timer" />.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Timer Finished
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a voice assistant timer has finished. The timer is available as `timer` of type <APIStruct text="voice_assistant::Timer" path="voice_assistant::Timer" />.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Timer Started
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a voice assistant timer has started. The timer is available as `timer` of type <APIStruct text="voice_assistant::Timer" path="voice_assistant::Timer" />.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Timer Tick
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant timers tick is triggered. This is called every **1 second** while there are timers on this device. The timers are available as `timers` which is a `std::vector` (array) of type <APIStruct text="voice_assistant::Timer" path="voice_assistant::Timer" />.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Timer Updated
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a voice assistant timer has been updated (paused/resumed/duration changed). The timer is available as `timer` of type <APIStruct text="voice_assistant::Timer" path="voice_assistant::Timer" />.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Tts End
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant has finished text-to-speech. A URL containing the audio response is available to automations as the variable `x`.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Tts Start
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the voice assistant has started text-to-speech. The text to be spoken is available to automations as the variable `x`.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Tts Stream End
triggers.description: An automation to perform when audio stream (voice response) playback ends. Requires `speaker` to be configured.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Tts Stream Start
triggers.description: An automation to perform when audio stream (voice response) playback starts. Requires `speaker` to be configured.

triggers.name: Voice Assistant → On Wake Word Detected
triggers.description: An automation to perform when the assist pipeline has detected a wake word.

triggers.name: Wiegand → On Key
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a key has been pressed on the pad. The key is in a variable called `x`.

triggers.name: Wiegand → On Raw
triggers.description: An automation to perform for any data sent by the device. The value is in a variable called `value`, the number of bits is in a variable called `bits`. Note that this will include parity bits as well and no parity checking is done.

triggers.name: Wiegand → On Tag
triggers.description: An automation to perform when a Wiegand-compatible card or a tag has been read by the device. The tag code is in a variable called `x`.

triggers.name: Wifi → On Connect
triggers.description: An action to be performed when a connection is established.

triggers.name: Wifi → On Disconnect
triggers.description: An action to be performed when the connection is dropped.

triggers.name: Zephyr Ble Server → On Numeric Comparison Request
triggers.description: An automation to compare the passkeys shown on the two BLE devices. See [`on_numeric_comparison_request`](https://esphome.io/components/zephyr_ble_server#on_numeric_comparison_request).

triggers.name: Zigbee → On Join
triggers.description: Automation to run when the device joins the network. In [Lambdas](https://esphome.io/automations/templates#config-lambda) you can get the join type with `x`. It is `true` for join and `false` for rejoin.

actions.name: Ags10 → New I2C Address
actions.description: I2C address of AGS10 can be modified, and it is possible to use multiple AGS10 sensors on one bus. After sending the command for address changing, the new address is saved and takes effect immediately even after power-off.

actions.name: Ags10 → Set Zero Point
actions.description: Zero-point of AGS10 has been calibrated before leaving factory. User can re-calibrate the zero-point as needed.

actions.name: Aic3204 → Set Auto Mute Mode
actions.description: This action sets the auto-mute mode of the AIC3204. See page 111 in the [reference manual](https://www.ti.com/lit/ml/slaa557/slaa557.pdf?ts=1727495047647&ref_url=ti.com) for more information.

actions.name: Alarm Control Panel → Arm Away
actions.description: This action arms the alarm in away mode. The `code` is required when *requires_code_to_arm* is *true*.

actions.name: Alarm Control Panel → Arm Home
actions.description: This action arms the alarm in home mode. The `code` is required when *requires_code_to_arm* is *true*.

actions.name: Alarm Control Panel → Arm Night
actions.description: This action arms the alarm in night mode. The `code` is required when *requires_code_to_arm* is *true*.

actions.name: Alarm Control Panel → Disarm
actions.description: This action disarms the alarm. The `code` is required when *codes* is not empty.

actions.name: Alarm Control Panel → Pending
actions.description: This action puts the alarm in pending state (the state before triggered after *pending_time*).

actions.name: Alarm Control Panel → Triggered
actions.description: This action puts the alarm in triggered state.

actions.name: Animation → Next Frame
actions.description: Moves the animation to the next frame. This is equivalent to the `id(my_animation).next_frame();` lambda call.

actions.name: Animation → Prev Frame
actions.description: Moves the animation to the previous frame. This is equivalent to the `id(my_animation).prev_frame();` lambda call.

actions.name: Animation → Set Frame
actions.description: Moves the animation to a specific frame. This is equivalent to the `id(my_animation).set_frame(frame);` lambda call.

actions.name: Api → Respond
actions.description: This action sends a response back to the client that called the user-defined action. It can report success/error status and optionally include JSON data.

actions.name: At581X → Reset
actions.description: Restart the sensor.

actions.name: At581X → Settings
actions.description: > [!WARNING] > The hardware frontend reset option is only required to reset the frontend in case it is struck, before sending the > new configuration. However, a frontend reset is always performed after changing the settings.

actions.name: Audio Adc → Set Mic Gain
actions.description: This action sets the (microphone) gain of the ADC.

actions.name: Audio Dac → Mute Off
actions.description: This action unmutes the output of the DAC.

actions.name: Audio Dac → Mute On
actions.description: This action mutes the output of the DAC.

actions.name: Audio Dac → Set Volume
actions.description: This action sets the output volume of the DAC.

actions.name: Binary Sensor → Invalidate State
actions.description: This action will invalidate the current state of the sensor. It is most useful with the Template binary sensor. After the state is invalidated, it will be reported to Home Assistant as `unknown`. Example:

actions.name: Ble → Disable
actions.description: This action turns off the BLE interface on demand.

actions.name: Ble → Enable
actions.description: This action turns on the BLE interface on demand.

actions.name: Ble Client → Ble Write
actions.description: This action triggers a write to a specified BLE characteristic. The write is attempted in a best-effort fashion and will only succeed if the `ble_client`  's connection has been established and the peripheral exposes the expected BLE service and characteristic. Execution of the automation block sequence resumes after the write has completed. A write failure will *not* stop execution of succeeding actions (this allows a disconnect to be executed, for example.)

actions.name: Ble Client → Connect
actions.description: This action is useful only for devices with `auto_connect: false` and allows a connection to be made from within an automation. Once connected other actions like `ble_write` can be used. This is useful where a BLE server needs only to be interacted with occasionally, and thus does not need a constant connection held.

actions.name: Ble Client → Disconnect
actions.description: This action disconnects a device that was connected with the `ble_client.connect` action. Execution of the automation block sequence resumes after the disconnect has completed.

actions.name: Ble Client → Numeric Comparison Reply
actions.description: This action triggers an authentication attempt after a numeric comparison.

actions.name: Ble Client → Passkey Reply
actions.description: This action triggers an authentication attempt using the specified `passkey`.

actions.name: Ble Client → Remove Bond
actions.description: This action removes a device from the security database and manages unpairing.

actions.name: Ble Server → Numeric Comparison Reply
actions.description: This action triggers an authentication attempt after a numeric comparison.

actions.name: Bluetooth Password → Set
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for setting the bluetooth password.

actions.name: Bm8563 → Read Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action) triggers a synchronization of the current system time from the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Bm8563 → Start Timer
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action) starts the RTC timer.

actions.name: Bm8563 → Write Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action) triggers a synchronization of the current system time to the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Button → Press
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for pressing a button in an Automation.

actions.name: Canbus → Send
actions.description: The CAN bus can transmit frames by means of the `canbus.send` action. There are several ways to use it:

actions.name: Cc1101 → Begin Rx
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) puts the radio into RX mode and switches `gdo0_pin` to input mode.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Begin Tx
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) puts the radio into TX mode and switches `gdo0_pin` to output mode.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Reset
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) resets the CC1101 chip and re-applies configuration.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Send Packet
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) transmits a packet. Only available when `packet_mode` is enabled.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Channel
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the channel number at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Channel Spacing
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the channel spacing at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Dc Blocking Filter
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) enables or disables the DC blocking filter at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Filter Bandwidth
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the receive filter bandwidth at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Frequency
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the operating frequency at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Fsk Deviation
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the FSK/GFSK frequency deviation at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Idle
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) puts the radio into an idle state.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set If Frequency
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the intermediate frequency at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Manchester
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) enables or disables Manchester encoding at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Modulation Type
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the modulation type at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Msk Deviation
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the MSK deviation index at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Output Power
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the transmission power at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Rx Attenuation
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the internal RX attenuation at runtime.

actions.name: Cc1101 → Set Symbol Rate
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the symbol rate at runtime.

actions.name: Climate → Control
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for setting parameters for climate devices.

actions.name: Cm1106 → Calibrate Zero
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) executes zero point calibration command on the sensor with the given ID.

actions.name: Component → Resume
actions.description: Using this action you can manually call the `start_poller()` method of a component.

actions.name: Component → Suspend
actions.description: Using this action you can manually call the `stop_poller()` method of a component.

actions.name: Component → Update
actions.description: Using this action you can manually call the `update()` method of a component.

actions.name: Cover → Close
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) closes the cover with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Cover → Control
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) is a more generic version of the other cover actions and allows all cover attributes to be set.

actions.name: Cover → Open
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) opens the cover with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Cover → Stop
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) stops the cover with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Cover → Toggle
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) toggles the cover with the given ID when executed, cycling through the states close/stop/open/stop... This allows the cover to be controlled by a single push button.

actions.name: Cs5460A → Restart
actions.description: This action can be used in automations to interrupt the current *computation cycle* and start a new one. This is useful if you're measuring multiple current/power values using a single CS5460A chip and a signal multiplexer. As an example you can measure the power usage on up to wall sockets in a house by using one voltage transformer and 16 current transformers, each on the cable leading to the corresponding wall socket. One side of all the current transformers connects directly to your CS5460A current input terminal (plus any filtering and protection circuitry as recommended in the datasheet), while the other side each connects to one channel of a CD74HC4067 analog multiplexer. The multiplexer's single signal pin then connects to the CS5460A's second current input terminal. Every time the CS5460A sensor publishes a new power value, an automation can switch the multiplexer to the next channel but it needs to interrupt the *computation cycle* automatically started when the previous one ended, and start a new cycle that uses current samples only from the new CD74HC4067 multiplexer channel.

actions.name: Datetime → Set
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for setting a datetime date state. The `date` provided can be in one of 3 formats:

actions.name: Datetime → Set
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for setting a datetime datetime state. The `datetime` provided can be in one of 3 formats:

actions.name: Deep Sleep → Allow
actions.description: This action allows the given deep sleep component to enter deep sleep, after previously being prevented.

actions.name: Deep Sleep → Enter
actions.description: This action makes the given deep sleep component enter deep sleep immediately.

actions.name: Deep Sleep → Prevent
actions.description: This action prevents the given deep sleep component from entering deep sleep. Useful for keeping the ESP active during data transfer or OTA updating (See note below for more information).

actions.name: Delay
actions.description: This action delays the execution of the next action in the action list by a specified time period.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Pause
actions.description: Pauses playback, playback can be resumed from the same position with `dfplayer.start`.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Play
actions.description: Plays a track.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Play Folder
actions.description: Plays files inside numbered folders, folders must be numbered from 1 and with leading zeros. Like `01`, `02`, ... etc. Files inside the folders must be numbered with two leading zeros, like `001.mp3`, `002.mp3`, ... etc. Folder numbers can range from 1 to 99 and file name from 1 to 255 or folder number from 1 to 10 and file number from 1 to 1000.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Play Mp3
actions.description: Plays a track inside the folder `mp3`. Files inside the folder must be numbered from 1 to 9999, like `0001.mp3`, `0002.mp3`, ... etc. The folder name needs to be `mp3`, placed under the SD card root directory, and the mp3 file name needs to be 4 digits, for example, "0001.mp3", placed under the mp3 folder. If you want, you can add additional text after the number in the filename, for example, `0001hello.mp3`, but must always be referenced by number only in yaml.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Play Next
actions.description: Starts playback of next track or skips to the next track.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Play Previous
actions.description: Plays the previously played track.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Random
actions.description: Randomly plays all tracks.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Reset
actions.description: Module reset.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Set Device
actions.description: Changes the device in use. Valid values are `TF_CARD` and `USB`.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Set Eq
actions.description: Changes audio equalization preset.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Set Volume
actions.description: Changes volume.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Sleep
actions.description: Enters sleep mode. Playback is stopped and the action `dfplayer.set_device: TF_CARD` should be send for playback to be enabled again.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Start
actions.description: Starts playing a track or resumes paused playback.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Stop
actions.description: Stops playback.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Volume Down
actions.description: Turn volume down.

actions.name: Dfplayer → Volume Up
actions.description: Turn volume up.

actions.name: Dfrobot Sen0395 → Reset
actions.description: Restart the sensor.

actions.name: Dfrobot Sen0395 → Settings
actions.description: > [!WARNING] > Each change to the configuration of the mmWave radar triggers a write to its internal flash/EEPROM. > Write cycles to this memory are limited, so avoid the practice of changing settings frequently. > Determine the appropriate settings for your device and avoid changing them unless absolutely necessary.

actions.name: Display Menu → Down
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for navigating down in a menu. The action is usually wired to a clockwise turn of a rotary encoder or to the lower button of the joystick.

actions.name: Display Menu → Enter
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for triggering a selected menu item, resulting in an action depending on the type of the item - entering a submenu, starting/stopping editing or triggering a command. The action is usually wired to a press button of a rotary encoder or to the center button of the joystick.

actions.name: Display Menu → Hide
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for hiding the menu. A hidden menu does not react to `draw()` and does not process navigation actions.

actions.name: Display Menu → Left
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) usually wired to the left button of the joystick. In the `joystick` mode it is used to set the previous value or to decrement the numeric one; depending on the `immediate_edit` flag entering the edit mode is required or not. If used in the `rotary` mode it exits the editing. In both modes it can be also used to navigate back one level when used with the `back` menu item.

actions.name: Display Menu → Right
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) usually wired to the right button of the joystick. In the `joystick` mode it is used to set the next value or to increment the numeric one; depending on the `immediate_edit` flag entering the edit mode is required or not. In both modes it can be also used to enter the submenu when used with the `menu` menu item.

actions.name: Display Menu → Show
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for showing an inactive menu. The state of the menu remains unchanged, i.e. the menu level shown at the moment it was hidden is restored, as is the selected item. The following snippet shows the menu if it is inactive, otherwise triggers the selected item.

actions.name: Display Menu → Show Main
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for showing the root level of the menu.

actions.name: Display Menu → Up
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for navigating up in a menu. The action is usually wired to an anticlockwise turn of a rotary encoder or to the upper button of the joystick.

actions.name: Ds1307 → Read Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) triggers a synchronization of the current system time from the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Ds1307 → Write Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) triggers a synchronization of the current system time to the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Sensor.Duty Time → Reset
actions.description: This action resets the duty time counter. Causes a sensor update. Does not affect the `last_time` sensor. In lambdas, you may use the `reset()` method.

actions.name: Sensor.Duty Time → Start
actions.description: This action starts/resume time tracking. In lambdas, you may use the `start()` method.

actions.name: Sensor.Duty Time → Stop
actions.description: This action suspends time tracking. Causes the sensor to be updated, including the `last_time` sensor. In lambdas, you may use the `stop()` method.

actions.name: Emontx → Send Command
actions.description: Send a command string to the EmonTx device via UART. A newline character is appended automatically.

actions.name: Esp32 Ble Tracker → Start Scan
actions.description: Start a Bluetooth scan. If there is a scan already in progress, then the action is ignored.

actions.name: Esp32 Ble Tracker → Stop Scan
actions.description: Stops the bluetooth scanning. It can be started again with the above start scan action.

actions.name: Output.Esp8266 Pwm → Set Frequency
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to manually change the frequency of an ESP8266 PWM channel at runtime. Use cases include controlling a passive buzzer (for pitch control).

actions.name: Esp Ldo → Voltage Adjust
actions.description: If the LDO is configured with ``adjustable: true``, the voltage can be updated at runtime:

actions.name: Espnow → Broadcast
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for sending a data packet over the espnow protocol to any device that is listening.

actions.name: Espnow → Peer Add
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) to add a new peer to the internal allowed peers list.

actions.name: Espnow → Peer Delete
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) to remove a known peer from the internal allowed peers list.

actions.name: Espnow → Send
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for sending a data packet over the espnow protocol.

actions.name: Espnow → Set Channel
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) to change the channel that espnow is sending and receiving on.

actions.name: Event → Trigger
actions.description: This action allows for the triggering of an event from within an automation.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Change I2C Address
actions.description: Changes the i2c address of the pump to the provided value. After the address is changed you must upload a new version of the ESPHome firmware with the updated I2C address for the pump to work.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Clear Calibration
actions.description: Clear the calibration values stored in the pump. You can check the calibration status by using the [Calibration Status](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-calibration_status_text_sensor) sensor.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Clear Total Volume Dosed
actions.description: Clear the values of the [Current Volume Dosed](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-current_volume_dosed_sensor), [Total Volume Dosed](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-total_volume_dosed_sensor) and [Absolute Total Volume Dosed](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-absolute_total_volume_dosed_sensor) sensors.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Dose Continuously
actions.description: Use this action in an [automations](https://esphome.io/automations) to have the peristaltic pump dose continuously at the [Maximum Flow Rate](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-max_flow_rate_sensor). The pump will automatically stop after 20 days of running in continuous mode.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Dose Volume
actions.description: Use this action in an [automations](https://esphome.io/automations) to have the peristaltic pump dose an specific volume (in milliliters) at the [Maximum Flow Rate](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-max_flow_rate_sensor). If the volume is negative the pump will run backwards.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Dose Volume Over Time
actions.description: Use this action in an [automations](https://esphome.io/automations) to have the peristaltic pump dose an specific `volume` (in milliliters) over the provided `duration` (in minutes). At the end of the time period the pump will have dosed the specified `volume`. If the volume is negative the pump will run backwards.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Dose With Constant Flow Rate
actions.description: Use this action in an [automations](https://esphome.io/automations) to have the peristaltic pump dose an specific `volume` (in milliliters) every minute for the provided `duration` (in minutes). At the end of the time period the pump will have dosed the specified `volume` times the `duration`. If the volume is negative the pump will run backwards.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Find
actions.description: Use this action to make the LED on the Pump control board to blink for a minute. The pump will not respond to any other action while the LED is blinking.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Pause Dosing
actions.description: Use this action to pause a Dosing command that was previously issued. To determine if the dosing is paused or not, you can use the [Is Paused](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-is_paused_binary_sensor) sensor. If the pump is currently paused, issuing this action again will unpause it.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Set Calibration Volume
actions.description: Use this action to calibrate the peristaltic pump. The EZO-PMP needs two forms of calibration: absolute volume and volume over time. You can check the calibration status by using the [Calibration Status](https://esphome.io/components/ezo_pmp#ezo_pmp-calibration_status_text_sensor) sensor. For the procedure on calibrating the pump check the datasheet.

actions.name: Ezo Pmp → Stop Dosing
actions.description: Use this action to stop the current Dosing command.

actions.name: Fan → Cycle Speed
actions.description: Increments through speed levels of the fan with the given ID when executed. If the fan's speed level is set to maximum when executed, fan will cycle off unless `off_speed_cycle` is set to `false`.

actions.name: Fan → Toggle
actions.description: Toggles the ON/OFF state of the fan with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Fan → Turn Off
actions.description: Turns the fan with the given ID off when executed.

actions.name: Fan → Turn On
actions.description: Turns the fan with the given ID on when executed.

actions.name: Fingerprint Grow → Aura Led Control
actions.description: Controls the Aura LED on the reader. Only available on select models. NOTE: The R503 has 2 variants with different LED colour options.

actions.name: Fingerprint Grow → Cancel Enroll
actions.description: Cancels the current fingerprint enrollment process. Triggers the `on_enrollment_failed` trigger.

actions.name: Fingerprint Grow → Delete
actions.description: Removes the enrolled fingerprint from the slot number defined.

actions.name: Fingerprint Grow → Delete All
actions.description: Removes all enrolled fingerprints.

actions.name: Fingerprint Grow → Enroll
actions.description: Starts the fingerprint enrollment process on the slot number defined.

actions.name: Fingerprint Grow → Led Control
actions.description: Turns on or off the LED on the reader. Only available on select models. If you have the R503 or R503-RGB use [`fingerprint_grow.aura_led_control` Action](https://esphome.io/components/fingerprint_grow#fingerprint_grow-aura_led_control) instead.

actions.name: Globals → Set
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to change the value of a `global` variable without having to use the lambda syntax.

actions.name: Grove Tb6612Fng → Break
actions.description: Set the motor channel to be on break mode which it ensure the wheel wont spin even if forced or pushed

actions.name: Grove Tb6612Fng → Change Address
actions.description: If you require connecting multiple boards at once, the address can be changed using this action. The address can be changed to a value in the range of `0x01 - 0x7f` inclusive.

actions.name: Grove Tb6612Fng → No Standby
actions.description: Set the board to be awake, every esphome is restarted the default mode is set to standby to ensure the motor wont spin accidentally

actions.name: Grove Tb6612Fng → Run
actions.description: Set the motor to spin by defining the direction and speed of the rotation, speed is a range from 0 to 255

actions.name: Grove Tb6612Fng → Standby
actions.description: Set the board to be on standby when is not used for a long time which reduces power consumptions and any jerking motion when stationary

actions.name: Grove Tb6612Fng → Stop
actions.description: Set the motor to stop motion but wont stop to spin in case there is a force pulling down, you would want to use break action if this is your case

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Beeper Off
actions.description: (supported only by hOn) This action disables beep feedback on every command sent to AC (keep in mind that this will not work for IR remote commands).

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Beeper On
actions.description: (supported only by hOn) This action enables beep feedback on every command sent to AC.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Display Off
actions.description: This action turns the AC display off.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Display On
actions.description: This action turns the AC display on.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Health Off
actions.description: Turn off health mode.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Health On
actions.description: Turn on health mode (UV light sterilization).

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Power Off
actions.description: This action turns AC power off.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Power On
actions.description: This action turns AC power on.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Power Toggle
actions.description: This action toggles AC power.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Set Horizontal Airflow
actions.description: (supported only by hOn) Set direction for horizontal airflow if the horizontal swing is disabled.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Set Vertical Airflow
actions.description: (supported only by hOn) Set direction for vertical airflow if the vertical swing is disabled.

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Start Self Cleaning
actions.description: (supported only by hOn) Start [self-cleaning](https://www.haier.com/in/blogs/beat-the-summer-heat-with-haier-self-cleaning-ac.shtml).

actions.name: Climate.Haier → Start Steri Cleaning
actions.description: (supported only by hOn) Start 56°C steri-cleaning.

actions.name: Fan.Hbridge → Brake
actions.description: Set all h-bridge pins high, shorting the fan/motor's windings and forcing the motor to actively stop.

actions.name: Hc8 → Calibrate
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) executes baseline calibration command on the sensor with the given ID.

actions.name: Hdc302X → Heater Off
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) disables the integrated heater on the sensor with the given ID. After turning off the heater, allow several minutes for the sensor to cool down before expecting accurate readings.

actions.name: Hdc302X → Heater On
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) enables the integrated heater on the sensor with the given ID. The heater can be used to remove condensation from the humidity sensor or to verify sensor functionality.

actions.name: Hlk Fm22X → Delete
actions.description: Removes the enrolled face from the slot number defined.

actions.name: Hlk Fm22X → Delete All
actions.description: Removes all enrolled faces.

actions.name: Hlk Fm22X → Enroll
actions.description: Starts the face enrollment process with a name and direction. To successfully enroll a face, you need to successfully and consecutively scan the face from all directions. A failure in one direction will require enrolling the face again from the start.

actions.name: Hlk Fm22X → Reset
actions.description: Resets the module. Can be useful after a failed enrollment or scan if the module isn't responding correctly. If this command fails it will mark the module as failed.

actions.name: Hlk Fm22X → Scan
actions.description: Scans and tries to match to an enrolled face. Triggers one of the on_face_scan triggers.

actions.name: Homeassistant → Action
actions.description: > [!NOTE] > Be sure to [follow the instructions above](https://esphome.io/components/api#api-actions) to tell Home Assistant to allow > your device to perform actions.

actions.name: Homeassistant → Event
actions.description: > [!NOTE] > Be sure to [follow the instructions above](https://esphome.io/components/api#api-actions) to tell Home Assistant to allow > your device to perform actions.

actions.name: Homeassistant → Tag Scanned
actions.description: > [!NOTE] > Be sure to [follow the instructions above](https://esphome.io/components/api#api-actions) to tell Home Assistant to allow > your device to make action calls.

actions.name: Http Request → Get
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a GET request.

actions.name: Ota.Http Request → Flash
actions.description: This action triggers the download and installation of the updated firmware from the configured URL. As it's an ESPHome [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions), it may be used in any ESPHome [automation(s)](https://esphome.io/automations).

actions.name: Http Request → Post
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a POST request.

actions.name: Http Request → Send
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a request.

actions.name: Hub75 → Set Brightness
actions.description: This action allows you to dynamically change the brightness of the display at runtime.

actions.name: If
actions.description: This action first evaluates the `condition:` and then either executes the `then:` branch if the condition returns true or the `else:` branch if the condition returns false.

actions.name: Sensor.Integration → Reset
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to reset the value of the integration sensor to zero. For example this can be used to reset the integration sensor to zero at midnight with a time-based automation.

actions.name: Sensor.Integration → Set Value
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to set the integration sensor to a specific value. For example, it can be used to set the sensor to the battery capacity when it is fully charged.

actions.name: Key Collector → Disable
actions.description: This action deactivates a `key_collector`. It will stop accepting keys and will clear any already collected ones. If there is more than one key collector, you will need to provide the `id` of the one to disable.

actions.name: Key Collector → Enable
actions.description: This action activates a `key_collector`. It will start accepting keys. If there is more than one key collector, you will need to provide the `id` of the one to enable.

actions.name: Lambda
actions.description: This action executes an arbitrary piece of C++ code (see [Lambda](https://esphome.io/automations/templates#config-lambda)).

actions.name: Output.Ledc → Set Frequency
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to manually change the frequency of an LEDC channel at runtime. Use cases include controlling a passive buzzer (for pitch control).

actions.name: Output.Libretiny Pwm → Set Frequency
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to manually change the frequency of a LibreTiny PWM channel at runtime. Use cases include controlling a passive buzzer (for pitch control).

actions.name: Light → Addressable Set
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to manually set a range of LEDs on an addressable light to a specific color.

actions.name: Light → Control
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) is a generic call to change the state of a light - it is essentially just a combination of the turn_on and turn_off calls.

actions.name: Light → Dim Relative
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to dim a light that supports brightness by a relative amount.

actions.name: Light → Toggle
actions.description: This action toggles a light with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Light → Turn Off
actions.description: This action turns a light with the given ID off when executed.

actions.name: Light → Turn On
actions.description: This action turns a light with the given ID on when executed.

actions.name: Lightwaverf → Send Raw
actions.description: Send the raw data that has been captured via the dump system

actions.name: Lock → Lock
actions.description: This action locks a lock with the given ID on when executed.

actions.name: Lock → Open
actions.description: This action opens (e.g. unlatch) a lock with the given ID off when executed.

actions.name: Lock → Unlock
actions.description: This action unlocks a lock with the given ID off when executed.

actions.name: Logger → Log
actions.description: Print a formatted message to the logs.

actions.name: Logger → Set Level
actions.description: Set the log level at runtime. The level can only be set to a level that is no less severe than the global log level.

actions.name: Lvgl → Display Set Rotation
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) rotates the LVGL display.

actions.name: Lvgl → Lv Tileview Tile T Update
Lvgl → Page Next
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) changes the page to the next/previous based on the configuration (pages with their `skip` option enabled are...skipped). Page changes will wrap around at the end.

actions.name: Lvgl → Page Show
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) shows a specific page (including pages with their `skip` option enabled).

actions.name: Lvgl → Pause
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) pauses the activity of LVGL, including rendering.

actions.name: Lvgl → Resume
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) resumes the activity of LVGL, including rendering.

actions.name: Lvgl → Style Update
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) allows changing/updating the properties of a style at run time. This can be used to implement dynamic themes, e.g. light/dark mode, or to change the appearance of widgets based on user interaction.

actions.name: Lvgl → Update
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) allows changing/updating the background styling of the LVGL display layers at runtime. You can style the `top_layer` and/or `bottom_layer` using any [style properties](https://esphome.io/components/lvgl#lvgl-styling).

actions.name: Lvgl → Widget Disable
actions.description: These [actions](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) are shorthands for toggling the `disabled` state of any widget (which controls the appearance of the corresponding *disabled* style set of the theme):

actions.name: Lvgl → Widget Focus
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) moves the input focus to the nominated widget. Used mainly with encoder inputs to select a specific widget to receive input events. It may also allow the focus to be frozen on that widget, or can be used to move the focus to the next or previous widget in the focus group.

actions.name: Lvgl → Widget Hide
actions.description: These [actions](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) are shorthands for toggling the `hidden` [flag](https://esphome.io/components/lvgl/widgets#lvgl-widget-flags) of any widget.

actions.name: Lvgl → Widget Redraw
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) redraws the entire screen, or optionally only selected widgets. It does not change any widget properties. It is mostly useful to redraw the screen after resuming LVGL from the paused state.

actions.name: Lvgl → Widget Refresh
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) re-evaluates all properties specified with lambdas in the specified widget's configuration. This offers an alternative technique to using the `lvgl.widget.update` action, which updates specified properties.

actions.name: Lvgl → Widget Update
actions.description: This powerful [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) allows changing/updating any widget's common [style property](https://esphome.io/components/lvgl#lvgl-styling), state (templatable) or [flag](https://esphome.io/components/lvgl/widgets#lvgl-widget-flags) on the fly.

actions.name: Max7219Digit → Intensity
actions.description: The intensity of the screen can be set "dynamically" with this action.

actions.name: Max7219Digit → Invert On
actions.description: This action `max7219digit.invert_on` will invert the display. So background pixels are on and text pixels are off. `max7219digit.invert_off` sets the display back to normal. The background pixels are only set at the next update, the pixels drawn in the various functions like print, line, etc. are directly influenced by the invert command.

actions.name: Max7219Digit → Reverse On
actions.description: With these actions you can reverse the display direction from left-to-right to right-to-left.

actions.name: Max7219Digit → Turn On
actions.description: The display can be switched on and off "dynamically" with the actions `max7219digit.turn_on` / `max7219digit.turn_off`.

actions.name: Media Player → Clear Playlist
actions.description: This action will clear the media player's playlist.

actions.name: Media Player → Enqueue
actions.description: This action will add the specified media to the media player's queue.

actions.name: Media Player → Group Join
actions.description: This action will join the media player to a group.

actions.name: Media Player → Mute
actions.description: This action will mute the media player.

actions.name: Media Player → Next
actions.description: This action will skip to the next track.

actions.name: Media Player → Pause
actions.description: This action pauses the current playback.

actions.name: Media Player → Play
actions.description: This action will resume playing the media player.

actions.name: Media Player → Play Media
actions.description: This action will start playing the specified media.

actions.name: Media Player → Previous
actions.description: This action will skip to the previous track.

actions.name: Media Player → Repeat All
actions.description: This action will set the media player to repeat all tracks.

actions.name: Media Player → Repeat Off
actions.description: This action will turn off repeat mode.

actions.name: Media Player → Repeat One
actions.description: This action will set the media player to repeat the current track.

actions.name: Media Player → Shuffle
actions.description: This action will enable shuffle mode.

actions.name: Media Player → Stop
actions.description: This action stops the current playback.

actions.name: Media Player → Toggle
actions.description: This action will pause or resume the current playback.

actions.name: Media Player → Turn Off
actions.description: This action will turn off the media player.

actions.name: Media Player → Turn On
actions.description: This action will turn on the media player.

actions.name: Media Player → Unmute
actions.description: This action will unmute the media player.

actions.name: Media Player → Unshuffle
actions.description: This action will disable shuffle mode.

actions.name: Media Player → Volume Down
actions.description: This action will decrease the volume of the media player.

actions.name: Media Player → Volume Set
actions.description: This action will set the volume of the media player.

actions.name: Media Player → Volume Up
actions.description: This action will increase the volume of the media player.

actions.name: Mhz19 → Abc Disable
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) disables automatic baseline calibration on the sensor with the given ID.

actions.name: Mhz19 → Abc Enable
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) enables automatic baseline calibration on the sensor with the given ID.

actions.name: Mhz19 → Calibrate Zero
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) executes zero point calibration command on the sensor with the given ID.

actions.name: Mhz19 → Detection Range Set
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) configures the detection range of the sensor with the given ID.

actions.name: Microphone → Capture
actions.description: This action will start capturing audio data from the microphone. The data will be passed to any components listening and will be available in the `on_data` trigger.

actions.name: Microphone → Mute
actions.description: This action will apply a software mute to the audio data from the microphone before passing it to any listening components.

actions.name: Microphone → Stop Capture
actions.description: This action will stop capturing audio data from the microphone.

actions.name: Microphone → Unmute
actions.description: This action will disable applying a software mute initiated with `microphone.mute`.

actions.name: Midea Ac → Beeper Off
actions.description: This action turn off beeper feedback.

actions.name: Midea Ac → Beeper On
actions.description: This action turn on beeper feedback.

actions.name: Midea Ac → Display Toggle
actions.description: This action toggle ac screen. Works via UART if supported or [Remote Transmitter](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter/).

actions.name: Midea Ac → Follow Me
actions.description: This action transmits an IR FollowMe command telling the air conditioner a more accurate room temperature value to be used instead of the internal indoor unit sensor.

actions.name: Midea Ac → Power Off
actions.description: This action turn off power.

actions.name: Midea Ac → Power On
actions.description: This action turn on power. The mode and preset will be restored to the last state before turned off.

actions.name: Midea Ac → Power Toggle
actions.description: This action toggle the power state. Identical to pressing the power button on the remote control.

actions.name: Midea Ac → Swing Step
actions.description: This action adjust the louver by one step. [Remote Transmitter](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter/) required.

actions.name: Mixer Speaker → Apply Ducking
actions.description: This action ducks (reduces the volume of) the media stream.

actions.name: Mqtt → Disable
actions.description: This action turns off the MQTT component on demand.

actions.name: Mqtt → Enable
actions.description: This action turns on the MQTT component on demand.

actions.name: Mqtt → Publish
actions.description: Publish an MQTT message on a topic using this action in automations.

actions.name: Mqtt → Publish Json
actions.description: Publish a JSON-formatted MQTT message on a topic using this action in automations.

actions.name: Binary Sensor.Nextion → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a Nextion binary sensor from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `binary_sensor.nextion.publish` action.

actions.name: Display.Nextion → Set Brightness
actions.description: You can use this [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#actions-action) to set the brightness of the Nextion's backlight.

actions.name: Sensor.Nextion → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a Nextion sensor from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `sensor.nextion.publish` action.

actions.name: Switch.Nextion → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a Nextion switch from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `switch.nextion.publish` action.

actions.name: Text Sensor.Nextion → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a Nextion text sensor from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `text_sensor.nextion.publish` action.

actions.name: Number → Decrement
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for decrementing a number value by its step size (default: 1).

actions.name: Number → Increment
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for incrementing a number value by its step size (default: 1).

actions.name: Number → Operation
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) that can be used to perform an operation on a number component (set to minimum or maximum value, decrement, increment), using a generic templatable action call.

actions.name: Number → Set
actions.description: You can also set the number for the template number from elsewhere in your YAML file with the [`number.set` Action](https://esphome.io/components/number#number-set_action).

actions.name: Number → To Max
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) setting a number to its maximum value, given a number component that has a maximum value defined for it.

actions.name: Number → To Min
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) setting a number to its minimum value, given a number component that has a minimum value defined for it.

actions.name: Online Image → Release
actions.description: Release the memory currently used by an image. Can be used if different display pages need different images, to avoid wasting memory on an image that is currently not being displayed.

actions.name: Online Image → Set Url
actions.description: Change the URL where the image is downloaded from. A re-download will be automatically triggered unless `update` is set to `false`.

actions.name: Output → Set Level
actions.description: This action sets the float output to the given level when executed.

actions.name: Output → Set Max Power
actions.description: This action sets the maximum output power level for the specified float output platform. It allows you to dynamically adjust the `max_power` configuration variable at runtime.

actions.name: Output → Set Min Power
actions.description: This action sets the minimum output power level for the specified float output platform. It allows you to dynamically adjust the `min_power` configuration variable at runtime.

actions.name: Output → Turn Off
actions.description: This action turns the output with the given ID off when executed.

actions.name: Output → Turn On
actions.description: This action turns the output with the given ID on when executed.

actions.name: Display → Show
actions.description: Show a specific page.

actions.name: Display → Show Next
actions.description: Shows the next or previous page, wraps around at the end.

actions.name: Pcf85063 → Read Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) triggers a synchronization of the current system time from the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Pcf85063 → Write Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) triggers a synchronization of the current system time to the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Pcf8563 → Read Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) triggers a synchronization of the current system time from the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Pcf8563 → Write Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) triggers a synchronization of the current system time to the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Climate.Pid → Autotune
actions.description: This action starts the autotune process of the PID controller.

actions.name: Climate.Pid → Reset Integral Term
actions.description: This action resets the integral term of the PID controller to 0. This might be necessary under certain conditions to avoid the control loop to overshoot (or undershoot) a target.

actions.name: Climate.Pid → Set Control Parameters
actions.description: This action sets new values for the control parameters of the PID controller. This can be used to manually tune the PID controller. Make sure to take update the values you want on the YAML file! They will reset on the next reboot.

actions.name: Output.Pipsolar → Set Level
actions.description: To use your outputs in [automations](https://esphome.io/automations) or templates, you can use this action to set the target level of the output.

actions.name: Pmwcs3 → Air Calibration
actions.description: The pmwcs3 probe can to be calibrated in dry/air conditions. The air calibration is a 30s procedure.

actions.name: Pmwcs3 → New I2C Address
actions.description: A new I2C address can be set (for multi-probes cases for example)

actions.name: Pmwcs3 → Water Calibration
actions.description: The pmwcs3 probe can to be also calibrated in water saturated conditions. Install the probe into a glass of water. The water calibration is also a 30s procedure.

actions.name: Pulse Counter → Set Total Pulses
actions.description: Using this action, you are able to reset/set the total pulse count. This can be useful if you would like the `total` sensor to match what you see on your meter you are trying to match.

actions.name: Pulse Meter → Set Total Pulses
actions.description: Using this action, you are able to reset/set the total pulse count. This can be useful if you would like the `total` sensor to match what you see on your meter you are trying to match.

actions.name: Pzemac → Reset Energy
actions.description: This action resets the total energy value of the pzemac device with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Pzemdc → Reset Energy
actions.description: This action resets the total energy value of the pzemdc device with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Digital Write
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the output value of the pin.

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Abbwelcome
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a ABB-Welcome message to the intercom bus. The message type, addresses, address length and data can vary a lot between ABB-Welcome systems. Please refer to the received messages while performing actions like ringing a doorbell or opening a door.

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Aeha
