actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a Samsung infrared remote code to a remote transmitter. It transmits codes up to 64 bits in length in a single packet.

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Samsung36
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a Samsung36 infrared remote code to a remote transmitter. It transmits the `address` and `command` in two packets separated by a "space".

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Sony
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) a Sony infrared remote code to a remote transmitter.

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Symphony
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action) sends a Symphony infrared remote code to a remote transmitter. It transmits constant bit-time frames with a footer gap. Physical Symphony remotes typically send the same frame twice separated by a ~35 ms gap. Use `command_repeats` to control how many identical frames are sent; defaults to 2.

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Toshiba Ac
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a Toshiba AC infrared remote code to a remote transmitter.

actions.name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Toto
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a Toto infrared remote code to a remote transmitter.

actions.name: Repeat
actions.description: This action allows you to repeat a block a given number of times. For example, the automation below will flash the light five times.

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Beep
actions.description: Activate the internal buzzer to make a beep.

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Learn
actions.description: Tell the RF Bridge to learn new protocol timings using this action in automations. A new code with timings will be returned to [`on_code_received` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/rf_bridge#rf_bridge-on_code_received)

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Send Advanced Code
actions.description: Send an RF code using this action in automations.

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Send Code
actions.description: Send a standard (0xA5) RF code using this action in automations.

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Send Raw
actions.description: Send a raw command to the onboard radio chip. The OEM RF firmware is able to raw send only standard signals (usually short), for other signals (B0 transmit), flashing the RF chip with Portisch or Mightymos firmware is needed.

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Start Advanced Sniffing
actions.description: Tell the RF Bridge to listen for the advanced/extra protocols defined in the portisch firmware. The decoded codes with length and protocol will be returned to [`on_advanced_code_received` Trigger](https://esphome.io/components/rf_bridge#rf_bridge-on_advanced_code_received)

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Start Bucket Sniffing
actions.description: Tell the RF Bridge to dump raw sniffing data. Useful for getting codes for unsupported protocols. The raw data will be available in the log and can later be used with [`rf_bridge.send_raw` Action](https://esphome.io/components/rf_bridge#rf_bridge-send_raw_action) action.

actions.name: Rf Bridge → Stop Advanced Sniffing
actions.description: Tell the RF Bridge to stop listening for the advanced/extra protocols defined in the portisch firmware.

actions.name: Sensor.Rotary Encoder → Set Value
actions.description: The internal state of the rotary encoder can be manually changed to any value with this action. After executing this action, rotating the encoder further will increase/decrease the state relative to the newly set internal value.

actions.name: Rtttl → Play
actions.description: Plays an RTTTL tone.

actions.name: Rtttl → Stop
actions.description: Stops playback.

actions.name: Rx8130 → Read Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action) triggers a synchronization of the current system time from the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Rx8130 → Write Time
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action) triggers a synchronization of the current system time to the RTC hardware.

actions.name: Safe Mode → Mark Successful
actions.description: This action marks the boot as successful, preventing safe mode from being invoked. This is useful for devices that take a sensor reading and then enter deep sleep, rather than waiting for the `boot_is_good_after` time to elapse.

actions.name: Scd4X → Factory Reset
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) triggers a factory reset of the sensor. Calibration settings are restored from factory settings.

actions.name: Scd4X → Perform Forced Calibration
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) manually calibrates the sensor to the provided value in ppm. Operate the SCD4x in the operation mode later used in normal sensor operation (periodic measurement, low power periodic measurement or single shot) for > 3 minutes in an environment with homogenous and constant CO2 concentration before performing a forced recalibration. As of March 2025, the global monthly mean CO₂ concentration is 426 ppm.

actions.name: Script → Execute
actions.description: This action executes the script. The script **mode** dictates what will happen if the script was already running.

actions.name: Script → Stop
actions.description: This action allows you to stop a given script during execution. If the script is not running, it does nothing. This is useful if you want to stop a script that contains a `delay` action, `wait_until` action, or is inside a `while` loop, etc. You can also call this action from the script itself, and any subsequent action will not be executed.

actions.name: Script → Wait
actions.description: This action suspends execution of the automation until a script has finished executing.

actions.name: Select → First
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for selecting the first option in a select component.

actions.name: Select → Last
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for selecting the last option in a select component.

actions.name: Select → Next
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for selecting the next option in a select component.

actions.name: Select → Operation
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) that can be used to change the active option in a select component (first, last, previous or next), using a generic templatable action call.

actions.name: Select → Previous
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for selecting the previous option in a select component.

actions.name: Select → Set
actions.description: You can also set an option for the template select from elsewhere in your YAML file with the [`select.set` Action](https://esphome.io/components/select#select-set_action).

actions.name: Select → Set Index
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for setting the active option using its index offset.

actions.name: Sen5X → Start Fan Autoclean
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) manually starts fan-cleaning.

actions.name: Sendspin → Media Source Disable Static Delay Adjustment
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) disables runtime adjustment of the static delay. The configured static delay is no longer applied to playback, and the Sendspin server cannot tune it.

actions.name: Sendspin → Media Source Enable Static Delay Adjustment
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) enables runtime adjustment of the static delay from the Sendspin server and applies the currently configured static delay value to playback.

actions.name: Sendspin → Switch
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) switches the Sendspin client to the next active group.

actions.name: Senseair → Abc Disable
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) disables Automatic Baseline Calibration on the sensor with the given ID.

actions.name: Senseair → Abc Enable
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) enables Automatic Baseline Calibration on the sensor with the given ID. ABC will be activated with the default interval of 180 hours.

actions.name: Senseair → Abc Get Period
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) requests the currently configured ABC interval from the sensor with the given ID. The value will be printed in ESPHome logs.

actions.name: Senseair → Background Calibration
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) initiates a background calibration on the sensor with the given ID: the current CO2 level will be used as a reference for the 400ppm threshold. Ensure that the sensor is in a stable environment with fresh ambient air, preferably near a window that has already been opened for a sufficient time.

actions.name: Senseair → Background Calibration Result
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) requests the result of the background calibration procedure from the sensor with the given ID. The value will be printed in ESPHome logs.

actions.name: Servo → Detach
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to disable the output on a servo motor - this will make the servo motor stop immediately and disable its active control.

actions.name: Servo → Write
actions.description: To use your servo motor in [automations](https://esphome.io/automations) or templates, you can use this action to set the target level of the servo from -100% to 100%.

actions.name: Sim800L → Connect
actions.description: Answers an incoming call.

actions.name: Sim800L → Dial
actions.description: Dial to a phone recipient using this action in automations.

actions.name: Sim800L → Disconnect
actions.description: Disconnects a call, either dialed in or received.

actions.name: Sim800L → Send Sms
actions.description: Send a SMS message to a phone recipient using this action in automations.

actions.name: Sim800L → Send Ussd
actions.description: Sends a ussd code to the network.

actions.name: Sound Level → Start
actions.description: Starts measuring sound levels. Does nothing in passive mode.

actions.name: Sound Level → Stop
actions.description: Stops measuring sound levels. Does nothing in passive mode.

actions.name: Speaker → Finish
actions.description: This action will stop playing audio data from the speaker after all data **is** played.

actions.name: Media Player.Speaker → Play On Device Media File
actions.description: This action will play a on-device media file.

actions.name: Speaker → Mute Off
actions.description: This action will unmute the speaker.

actions.name: Speaker → Mute On
actions.description: This action will mute the speaker.

actions.name: Speaker → Play
actions.description: This action will start playing raw audio data from the speaker.

actions.name: Speaker → Stop
actions.description: This action will stop playing audio data from the speaker and discard the unplayed data.

actions.name: Speaker → Volume Set
actions.description: This action will set the volume of the speaker.

actions.name: Speaker Source → Set Playlist Delay
actions.description: This action sets the delay between consecutive tracks in a pipeline's playlist. This can be useful to add a pause between announcement files or between media tracks.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Clear Queued Valves
actions.description: Removes all queued valves from the controller's queue. Please see [The Sprinkler Controller Queue](https://esphome.io/components/sprinkler#sprinkler-controller-sprinkler_controller_queue) section below for more detail and examples.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Next Valve
actions.description: Advances to the next valve (numerically). If `manual_selection_delay` is configured, the controller will wait before activating the selected valve. If no valve is active, the first valve (as they appear in the controller's configuration) will be started. Setting `next_prev_ignore_disabled` to `true` will cause this action to skip valves that are not enabled via their valve enable switch (see above).

actions.name: Sprinkler → Pause
actions.description: Immediately turns off all valves, saving the active valve and the amount of time remaining so that the cycle may be resumed later on.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Previous Valve
actions.description: Advances to the previous valve (numerically). If `manual_selection_delay` is configured, the controller will wait before activating the selected valve. If no valve is active, the last valve (as they appear in the controller's configuration) will be started. Setting `next_prev_ignore_disabled` to `true` will cause this action to skip valves that are not enabled via their valve enable switch (see above).

actions.name: Sprinkler → Queue Valve
actions.description: Adds the specified valve into the controller's queue. When the queue is enabled, valves in the queue take precedence over valves scheduled as a part of a full cycle of the system (when auto-advance is enabled). If `run_duration` is not specified or is zero, the sprinkler controller will use the valve's configured run duration. Valves are numbered in the order they appear in the sprinkler controller's configuration starting at zero (0). *Note that, at present, the queue has a hard-coded limit of 100 entries to limit memory use.* Please see [The Sprinkler Controller Queue](https://esphome.io/components/sprinkler#sprinkler-controller-sprinkler_controller_queue) section below for more detail and examples.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Resume
actions.description: Resumes a cycle placed on hold with `sprinkler.pause`. If there is no paused cycle, this action will do nothing.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Resume Or Start Full Cycle
actions.description: Resumes a cycle placed on hold with `sprinkler.pause`, but if no cycle was paused, starts a full cycle (equivalent to `sprinkler.start_full_cycle`  ).

actions.name: Sprinkler → Set Divider
actions.description: The divider value sets both the multiplier and repeat values as follows:

actions.name: Sprinkler → Set Multiplier
actions.description: Sets the multiplier value used to proportionally increase or decrease the run duration for all valves/zones. For seasonal changes, it's easier to use the multiplier to adjust the watering time instead of adjusting the run durations directly. Set your run duration to the time you want for 'mild' weather (spring/autumn) and then use the multiplier to increase (summer) or decrease (winter) the calculated run times. When a given valve is activated, this value is multiplied by the valve's run duration (see below) to determine the valve's actual run duration. *Note that a multiplier value of zero is allowed; if the multiplier value is zero, the sprinkler controller will not start any valves.* **This can result in confusing/unexpected behavior if a visual indication of this condition is not available!**

actions.name: Sprinkler → Set Repeat
actions.description: Specifies the number of times full cycles should be repeated. **Note that the total number of cycles the controller will run is equal to the repeat value plus one.** For example, with a `repeat` value of 1, the initial cycle will run, then the repeat cycle will run, resulting in a total of two cycles.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Set Valve Run Duration
actions.description: Sets the run duration for the specified valve. When the valve is activated, this value is multiplied by the multiplier value (see above) to determine the valve's actual run duration.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Shutdown
actions.description: Initiates a shutdown of all valves/the system, respecting any configured pump or valve stop delays.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Start From Queue
actions.description: Starts the controller running valves from its queue. If no valves are in the queue, this action does nothing; otherwise, this disables the controller's "auto-advance" feature so that only queued valves/zones will run. Queued valves will remain on for either the amount of time specified in the queue request or for their configured `run_duration` multiplied by the controller's multiplier value (if the queue request run duration is not specified or is zero). *Note that queued valves ignore whether the valve is enabled; that is, queued valves will always run once the controller is started, unless, of course, the queue is (manually) cleared prior to the queue reaching them. Also note that, at present, the queue has a hard-coded limit of 100 entries to limit memory use.* See [The Sprinkler Controller Queue](https://esphome.io/components/sprinkler#sprinkler-controller-sprinkler_controller_queue) section below for more detail.

actions.name: Sprinkler → Start Full Cycle
actions.description: Starts a full cycle of the system. This enables the controller's "auto-advance" feature and disables the queue. The controller will iterate through all enabled valves/zones. They will each run for their configured `run_duration` multiplied by the controller's multiplier value. *Note that if NO valves are enabled when this action is called, the controller will automatically enable all valves.*

actions.name: Sprinkler → Start Single Valve
actions.description: Starts a single valve. This disables the controller's "auto-advance" and queue features so that only this valve/zone will run. The valve will remain on for the specified duration or (if `run_duration` is not specified or is zero) for its configured `run_duration` multiplied by the controller's multiplier value. *Note that this action ignores whether the valve is enabled; that is, when called, the specified valve will always run.* Valves are numbered in the order they appear in the sprinkler controller's configuration starting at zero (0).

actions.name: Sps30 → Start Fan Autoclean
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) manually starts fan-cleaning.

actions.name: Sps30 → Start Measurement
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) manually puts the sensor into measurement mode.

actions.name: Sps30 → Stop Measurement
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) manually puts the sensor into idle mode.

actions.name: Stepper → Report Position
actions.description: All steppers start out with a target and current position of `0` on boot. However, if you for example want to home a stepper motor, it can be useful to **report** the stepper where it is currently at.

actions.name: Stepper → Set Acceleration
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to set the acceleration of a stepper at runtime.

actions.name: Stepper → Set Deceleration
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to set the deceleration of a stepper at runtime.

actions.name: Stepper → Set Speed
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to set the speed of a stepper at runtime.

actions.name: Stepper → Set Target
actions.description: To use your stepper motor in [automations](https://esphome.io/automations) or templates, you can use this action to set the target position (in steps). The stepper will always run towards the target position and stop once it has reached the target.

actions.name: Switch → Control
actions.description: This action allows you to control a switch with more flexibility than the basic `turn_on` and `turn_off` actions. It accepts a templatable `state` parameter, making it useful when the desired switch state is determined dynamically.

actions.name: Switch → Toggle
actions.description: This action toggles a switch with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Switch → Turn Off
actions.description: This action turns a switch with the given ID off when executed.

actions.name: Switch → Turn On
actions.description: This action turns a switch with the given ID on when executed.

actions.name: Sx126X → Run Image Cal
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) runs the `sx126x` image calibration.

actions.name: Sx126X → Send Packet
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a packet.

actions.name: Sx126X → Set Mode Rx
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx126x` mode to rx.

actions.name: Sx126X → Set Mode Sleep
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx126x` mode to sleep. By default the radio enters warm sleep — config registers are retained so that on wake it is ready for `sx126x.set_mode_rx` / `sx126x.set_mode_tx` again. For battery-powered nodes that pair the radio with deep sleep, `cold: true` switches to cold sleep (~0.6 µA, vs ~600 µA warm). Cold sleep does not retain config, so the radio must be reinitialized on wake. With deep sleep this happens automatically — the device reboots on wake and the component reinitializes the radio during startup.

actions.name: Sx126X → Set Mode Standby
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx126x` mode to standby.

actions.name: Sx126X → Set Mode Tx
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx126x` mode to tx.

actions.name: Sx127X → Run Image Cal
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) runs the `sx127x` image calibration, must be in standby mode.

actions.name: Sx127X → Send Packet
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a packet, the `sx127x` needs to be in packet mode.

actions.name: Sx127X → Set Mode Rx
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx127x` mode to rx.

actions.name: Sx127X → Set Mode Sleep
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx127x` mode to sleep.

actions.name: Sx127X → Set Mode Standby
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx127x` mode to standby.

actions.name: Sx127X → Set Mode Tx
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sets the `sx127x` mode to tx.

actions.name: Tag → Emulation Off
actions.description: Use this action to disable card (tag) emulation mode. The PN7160 will no longer respond to requests from other readers, such as smartphones.

actions.name: Tag → Emulation On
actions.description: Use this action to enable card (tag) emulation mode. The PN7160 will respond to requests from other readers, such as smartphones.

actions.name: Tag → Polling Off
actions.description: Use this action to disable card (tag) reading/writing. The PN7160 will no longer read or write cards/tags.

actions.name: Tag → Polling On
actions.description: Use this action to enable card (tag) reading/writing. The PN7160 will read or write cards/tags.

actions.name: Tag → Set Clean Mode
actions.description: Use this action to invoke "clean mode" -- the next tag presented to the PN7160 will be "cleaned", removing all data from the tag.

actions.name: Tag → Set Emulation Message
actions.description: Use this action to set the NDEF message used for card (tag) emulation mode, when enabled (see below).

actions.name: Tag → Set Format Mode
actions.description: Use this action to invoke "format mode" -- the next tag presented to the PN7160 will be "formatted", leaving only an empty NDEF message structure on the tag.

actions.name: Tag → Set Read Mode
actions.description: Use this action to invoke "read mode" -- the next tag presented to the PN7160 will be read. This is the default mode that the component operates in.

actions.name: Tag → Set Write Message
actions.description: Use this action to set the NDEF message used for "write mode" (see below).

actions.name: Tag → Set Write Mode
actions.description: Use this action to invoke "write mode" -- the next tag presented to the PN7160 will have its NDEF message set to the message defined by the `tag.set_write_message` action (see above). **Note that a message must be set before this mode may be invoked.**

actions.name: Binary Sensor.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a template binary sensor from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `binary_sensor.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Cover.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a template cover from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `cover.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Lock.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a template lock from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `lock.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Sensor.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a template sensor from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `sensor.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Switch.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a template switch from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `switch.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Text Sensor.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a template text sensor from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `text_sensor.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Valve.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish a state to a template valve from elsewhere in your YAML filewith the `valve.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Water Heater.Template → Publish
actions.description: You can also publish state to a template water heater from elsewhere in your YAML file with the `water_heater.template.publish` action.

actions.name: Text → Set
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for setting a text state.

actions.name: Datetime → Set
actions.description: This is an [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) for setting a datetime time state. The `time` provided can be in one of 3 formats:

actions.name: Tm1651 → Set Brightness
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) allows you to manually change the brightness of the battery display at runtime.

actions.name: Tm1651 → Set Level
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the level of the battery display at runtime.

actions.name: Tm1651 → Set Level Percent
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) changes the level of the battery display at runtime. Automatically calculates input level in percentages to actual level for the display.

actions.name: Tm1651 → Turn Off
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) turns off all the LEDs.

actions.name: Tm1651 → Turn On
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) turns on the LEDs.

actions.name: Uart → Write
actions.description: This [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a defined UART signal to the given UART bus.

actions.name: Udp → Write
actions.description: To write data to the UDP port, use the `udp.write` action. This action takes a single argument, the data to write to the UDP port.

actions.name: Ufire Ec → Calibrate Probe
actions.description: The EC probe have to be calibrated. For this you need know the EC reference value and temperature of the calibration solution.

actions.name: Ufire Ec → Reset
actions.description: Reset the current calibration on the sensor.

actions.name: Ufire Ise → Calibrate Probe High
actions.description: The pH probe have to be calibrated. For this you need to know the pH reference value of the calibration high solution.

actions.name: Ufire Ise → Calibrate Probe Low
actions.description: The pH probe have to be calibrated. For this you need to know the pH reference value of the calibration low solution.

actions.name: Ufire Ise → Reset
actions.description: Reset the current calibration on the sensor.

actions.name: Update → Check
actions.description: This action allows you to trigger the update entity to check for, but not install, updates.

actions.name: Update → Perform
actions.description: This action allows you to trigger the update entity to start the update process.

actions.name: Valve → Close
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) closes the valve with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Valve → Control
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) is a more generic version of the other valve actions and allows all valve attributes to be set.

actions.name: Valve → Open
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) opens the valve with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Valve → Stop
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) stops the valve with the given ID when executed.

actions.name: Valve → Toggle
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) toggles the valve with the given ID when executed, cycling through the states close/stop/open/stop... This allows the valve to be controlled by a single push button.

actions.name: Voice Assistant → Start
actions.description: Listens for one voice command then stops.

actions.name: Voice Assistant → Start Continuous
actions.description: Start listening for voice commands. This will start listening again after the response audio has finished playing. Some errors will stop the cycle. Call `voice_assistant.stop` to stop the cycle.

actions.name: Voice Assistant → Stop
actions.description: Stop listening for voice commands.

actions.name: Wait until
actions.description: This action allows your automations to wait until a condition evaluates to true. (So this is just a shorthand way of writing a `while` action with an empty `then` block.)

actions.name: While
actions.description: This action is similar to the [if](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#if_action) Action. The `while` action loops through a block as long as the given condition is true.

actions.name: Wifi → Configure
actions.description: This action connects to an SSID and password, optionally saving it in persistent memory so that the next time the WiFi interface is enabled, it will connect to the stored access point.

actions.name: Wifi → Disable
actions.description: This action turns off the WiFi interface on demand.

actions.name: Wifi → Enable
actions.description: This action turns on the WiFi interface on demand.

actions.name: Wireguard → Disable
actions.description: This action drops down the active VPN link (if any) and disables the component.

actions.name: Wireguard → Enable
actions.description: This action enables the component and starts the connection to the remote peer.

actions.name: Zigbee → Factory Reset
actions.description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action) triggers a factory reset of the Zigbee device. It handles leaving the Zigbee network.

conditions.name: Alarm Control Panel → Is Armed
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the alarm control panel is armed.

conditions.name: And
conditions.description: Check a combination of conditions. `all` is a synonym for `and`, and `any` is a synonym for `or`. `all` and `any` may also be used directly in place of `condition`.

conditions.name: Api → Connected
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if at least one client is connected to the ESPHome native API.

conditions.name: Binary Sensor → Is On
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the given binary sensor is ON (or OFF).

conditions.name: Ble → Enabled
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if BLE is currently enabled or not.

conditions.name: Component → Is Idle
conditions.description: This condition checks if a given component is idle. A component is considered to be idle if it has completed setup, has not been marked as failed, and is not currently being called by the loop task. This is useful for synchronizing actions with the state of the component, for example, an e-paper display component that requires a significant amount of time to update the display panel.

conditions.name: Cover → Is Closed
conditions.description: This [condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the cover with the given ID is fully closed.

conditions.name: Cover → Is Open
conditions.description: This [condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the cover with the given ID is fully open.

conditions.name: Dfplayer → Is Playing
conditions.description: This Condition returns true while playback is active.

conditions.name: Display → Is Displaying Page
conditions.description: This condition returns true while the specified page is being shown.

conditions.name: Display Menu → Is Active
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the given menu is active, i.e. shown on the display and processing navigation events.

conditions.name: Sensor.Duty Time → Is Running
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the `duty_time` counter is currently running (or suspended). In lambdas, you may use the `is_running()` method.

conditions.name: Fan → Is On
conditions.description: This [condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) passes if the given fan is on or off.

conditions.name: For
conditions.description: Allows you to check if a given condition has been true for at least a given amount of time.

conditions.name: Lambda
conditions.description: This condition performs an arbitrary piece of C++ code (see [Lambda](https://esphome.io/automations/templates#config-lambda)) and can be used to create conditional flow in actions.

conditions.name: Light → Is On
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the given light is ON or OFF. OFF means that the light is completely OFF, and ON means that the light is emitting at least a bit of light.

conditions.name: Lock → Is Locked
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the given lock is LOCKED (or UNLOCKED).

conditions.name: Lvgl → Is Idle
conditions.description: This [condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#common_conditions) checks if the amount of time specified has passed since the last touch event.

conditions.name: Lvgl → Is Paused
conditions.description: This [condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#common_conditions) checks if LVGL is in the paused state or not.

conditions.name: Lvgl → Page Is Showing
conditions.description: This [condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#common_conditions) checks if the nominated page is the one currently showing.

conditions.name: Media Player → Is Announcing
conditions.description: This condition checks if the media player is playing an announcement.

conditions.name: Media Player → Is Idle
conditions.description: This condition checks if the media player is idle.

conditions.name: Media Player → Is Muted
conditions.description: This condition checks if the media player is muted.

conditions.name: Media Player → Is Off
conditions.description: This condition checks if the media player is turned off.

conditions.name: Media Player → Is On
conditions.description: This condition checks if the media player is turned on.

conditions.name: Media Player → Is Paused
conditions.description: This condition checks if the media player is paused.

conditions.name: Media Player → Is Playing
conditions.description: This condition checks if the media player is playing media.

conditions.name: Micro Wake Word → Model Is Enabled
Microphone → Is Capturing
conditions.description: This condition will check if the microphone is currently capturing audio data.

conditions.name: Microphone → Is Muted
conditions.description: This condition will check if the microphone is currently apply a software mute.

conditions.name: Mqtt → Connected
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the MQTT client is currently connected to the MQTT broker.

conditions.name: Number → In Range
conditions.description: This condition passes if the state of the given number is inside a range.

conditions.name: Rtttl → Is Playing
conditions.description: This Condition returns true while playback is active.

conditions.name: Script → Is Running
conditions.description: This [condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) allows you to check if a given script is running. In case scripts are run in `parallel`, this condition only tells you if at least one script of the given id is running, not how many. Not designed for use with [while](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#while_action); instead try [script.wait](https://esphome.io/components/script#script-wait_action).

conditions.name: Select → Is
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the select is set to any one of a list of options. A lambda may also be used for more complex computations.

conditions.name: Sensor → In Range
conditions.description: This condition passes if the state of the given sensor is inside a range.

conditions.name: Speaker → Is Playing
conditions.description: This condition will check if the speaker is currently playing audio data.

conditions.name: Speaker → Is Stopped
conditions.description: This condition will check if the speaker is fully stopped audio data and is in idle mode.

conditions.name: Switch → Is On
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the given switch is ON (or OFF).

conditions.name: Text Sensor → State
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) allows you to check if a given text sensor has a specific state.

conditions.name: Time → Has Time
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if time has been set and is valid.

conditions.name: Update → Is Available
conditions.description: This condition checks if an update is available for the device.

conditions.name: Voice Assistant → Connected
conditions.description: Returns true if the voice assistant is currently connected to Home Assistant.

conditions.name: Voice Assistant → Is Running
conditions.description: Returns true if the voice assistant is currently running.

conditions.name: Wifi → Ap Active
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if WiFi AP is currently active or not.

conditions.name: Wifi → Connected
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if the WiFi client is currently connected to a station.

conditions.name: Wifi → Enabled
conditions.description: This [Condition](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-conditions) checks if WiFi is currently enabled or not.

conditions.name: Wireguard → Enabled
conditions.description: This condition checks if WireGuard® is currently enabled or not.

conditions.name: Wireguard → Peer Online
conditions.description: This condition checks if the remote peer is *online*.
